name: Blackened Chicken
recipeIngredient: 16 skinless boneless chicken breasts (about 3 ounces each)
3/4 pound unsalted butter, melted and kept warm
recipeInstructions:
Note: Breasts should be 3 ounces each, about 1/2 to 3/4 inch thick. Alternatively, use 8 bone-in leg-thigh pieces or a combination of both.
Skin the leg-thigh pieces and remove bones from each piece, leaving meat in one piece. Trim excess fat. Pound each breast or leg-thigh fillet to 1/2 inch thick.
1. Let the chicken reach room temperature before blackening.
2. Thoroughly combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl.
3. Heat a large cast-iron skillet over very high heat until extremely hot and just short of white ash forming on the bottom (about 8 minutes). The cooking time may vary depending on the heat source intensity.
4. Preheat serving plates in an oven set to 250°F.
5. Before cooking each piece, dip it in melted butter so both sides are well coated. Sprinkle each fillet evenly with the seasoning mix using about a rounded 1/2 teaspoon, patting it in with your hands.
6. Place the fillet skin side down in the hot skillet, ensuring all meat folds are opened and the meat is lying flat. Pour about 1 teaspoon butter on top of the fillet (be cautious as the butter may flare up). If cooking more than one fillet at a time, place each fillet in the skillet before adding butter and seasoning to the next.
7. Cook uncovered over the same high heat until the underside forms a crust, about 2 minutes. The cooking time will vary depending on fillet thickness and skillet/fryer temperature; watch for a white line forming as it cooks.
8. Turn the fillets over and pour about 1 teaspoon more melted butter on top of each. Cook just until meat is thoroughly cooked, about 2 more minutes.
9. Serve chicken fillets crust side up while hot.
10. Clean the skillet after cooking each batch and repeat the blackening procedure with remaining chicken fillets.
To serve, place 2 breast fillets or 1 leg-thigh fillet on each heated serving plate. If using a large serving platter, do not stack the fillets.
